name: Stewed Chicken
recipeInstructions:
Provides chicken and stock for four meals
1. Cut the chicken into manageable pieces. Remove skin and bones from breast halves. Save breast halves for Braised Chicken Rolls (p. 36).
2. Place remaining chicken parts, including skin and bones from breast halves, in a saucepan.
3. Add vegetables, pepper, and water to the saucepan. Bring mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and cover.
4. Simmer until chicken is tender, about 45 minutes.
5. Remove chicken and vegetables from stock. Pour remaining stock into a measuring cup, leaving as much fat layer as possible.
6. Press vegetables through a strainer (or mash with fork) and add to the defatted stock. If necessary, add water to make 2 cups total. Save 2/3 cup for Braised Chicken Rolls (p. 36) and 1 cup for Chicken Macaroni Stew (p. 39).
7. Separate meat from skin and bones. Dice and save for recipes.
NOTE: Only part of the chicken is used in Week 2 menus. Freeze remaining cooked meat and stock for later use.
Thrifty Meals for Two: Making Food Dollars Count
USDA Home and Garden Bulletin Number 244
